content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Accept the concrete parsing defects that currently misreport contract violations or misclassify bead-description scope. Reject the two amendments that would remove intentional prompt-override opt-out behavior or conflict with the design choice to preserve verbatim `AGENTS / Repo Guidance` content.

## Votes

- `fr-5-7eb75cd0`: Accept
  The current validator only records a heading when it exactly matches `expected_index`. Once a section is skipped, later canonical headings are neither marked as seen nor allowed to advance the cursor, so one omission cascades into repeated `unexpected` errors plus final `missing` errors for headings that were actually present. Advancing past skipped sections when a later heading is found fixes misleading diagnostics without weakening rejection of invalid prompts.
- `fr-5-d580dd6c`: Accept
  `split_bead_description_scope` has no fenced-code tracking, so literal examples inside ```/~~~ blocks can be mistaken for real `Non-goals` or `Acceptance Criteria` boundaries. That corrupts the rendered prompt by extracting content from code examples as if it were task structure. Fence-aware boundary detection is the correct and robust fix.
- `fr-5-dcf32291`: Reject
  The contract is intentionally marker-based today. A `--prompt-file` override without the marker is treated as a generic custom prompt, and downstream consumers consistently use the same `prompt_uses_contract(...)` gate. Requiring every bead-backed override to carry the canonical contract would remove an existing escape hatch and change supported override semantics; that is a design change, not a correctness defect in the current implementation.
- `fr-5-455d99e1`: Reject
  After `## AGENTS / Repo Guidance`, the remainder of the document is intentionally treated as verbatim embedded guidance. The current validator and tests explicitly allow canonical-looking headings there so imported AGENTS content is not rejected or rewritten. Rejecting any later canonical heading would break that behavior; if stronger disambiguation is wanted, it needs a different delimiting scheme rather than this validator change.
- `fr-5-0405b9a2`: Accept
  While inside `NonGoals` or `AcceptanceCriteria`, the parser currently clears section state on any markdown heading, so subsection headings like `### Examples` cause later bullets to fall back into `Must-Do Scope`. That materially changes task semantics by turning explicit non-goals or acceptance notes into in-scope work. Section mode should only end on an actual recognized section boundary.
